response.justification: The candidate is an experienced generalist with strong web, full-stack, and some low-level (DLang, C/C++) background, but misses the role's hard requirements: there is no indication of native-level Japanese proficiency (languages listed are English, Romanian, basic German), no Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience, and no exposure to embedded networking, LAN switches, or security technologies like MACsec or 802.1X. C for UNIX experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments. The profile does not fit this embedded security position.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput services) shows systems-level aptitude
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — the role requires native-level Japanese
No Linux kernel analysis/modification, embedded networking, or L2/L3 switch experience
Minimal professional C experience and no background in security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption, tamper detection)

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security-domain experience
